title: Algebraic K-Theory Via Binary Complexes
speaker: Daniel Grayson
affiliation: University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign; Member, School of Mathematics

proof_stages.label: Quillen K-theory and the two-proofs heuristic
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls K0 by generators and relations in an exact category, Quillen's higher K-groups via the geometric realization of Q(M), and motivates the principle that two proofs of vanishing in degree n should produce a class in degree n+1.

proof_stages.label: From two K0 proofs to a double exact sequence
proof_stages.summary: Applying the heuristic to the Grothendieck group, two formal exact-sequence proofs are subtracted and reorganized by direct sums and permutation isomorphisms into two unrelated short exact sequences on the same three objects.

proof_stages.label: Q-construction sphere from a double exact sequence
proof_stages.summary: Quillen's Q-category is unpacked so that each short exact sequence gives a 2-simplex filling whose boundary depends only on its three objects, hence two such fillings glue to an S^2 class in BQ(M), i.e. an element of K1.

proof_stages.label: Acyclic binary complexes and good exact categories
proof_stages.summary: The short binary object is lengthened to bounded acyclic binary chain complexes, with mapping cones forcing longer lengths and a mild ambient-abelian 'good category' hypothesis ensuring long exact sequences and homological algebra behave as needed.

proof_stages.label: The Omega category as a diagonal quotient
proof_stages.summary: Bounded acyclic chain complexes embed diagonally into bounded acyclic binary complexes, and treating this embedding as a pair of exact categories defines Omega(M), killing the trivial case where the two proofs are identical.

proof_stages.label: Loop-space theorem and iteration
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem identifies K(Omega(M)) with the loop space of K(M) after discarding K0, and iterating gives K0(Omega^n(M)) isomorphic to K_n(M).

proof_stages.label: Generators and relations by binary multicomplexes
proof_stages.summary: Unwinding K0(Omega^n(M)) yields generators as n-dimensional bounded graded objects with two acyclic differentials in each direction, subject to commutativity, short-exact-sequence relations, and vanishing when the two differentials agree in some direction.

proof_stages.label: Low-length cases, applications, and limitations
proof_stages.summary: The closing discussion describes length-two reductions, the length-one link with automorphisms and Steinberg symbols, applications to motivic and relative K-theory, and current difficulty recovering Quillen's foundational theorems from this presentation.

examples.note: Finitely generated modules, projective modules, and free modules over a ring are used to make exact categories and the later 'good category' hypothesis concrete.

examples.name: Two nullhomologies in a chain complex
examples.note: For a cycle z=d w=d w' in a chain complex of abelian groups, the difference w-w' is a higher cycle, producing the guiding 'two proofs give one higher class' idea.

examples.name: Two nullhomotopies glued along the boundary
examples.note: Two extensions of a map S^n -> X over the ball glue to a map S^{n+1} -> X, giving the homotopy-theoretic version of the guiding principle.

examples.name: Length-one binary complex as an automorphism
examples.note: When a binary complex has length one, both differentials are isomorphisms and d^{-1}d' gives an automorphism, illustrating the generators by the familiar K1 class of an invertible matrix.

examples.name: Steinberg symbol from a square of units
examples.note: A length-one two-dimensional case with two units is identified with a Steinberg symbol in K2, serving as a familiar low-dimensional test case and later as an example of relations still hard to prove in this framework.
